content:
- Completed task 10 end-to-end: added new `src/test_harness` module family (`namespace`, `ports`, `pg16`, `etcd3`, `tls`, `auth`) and wired it under `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-harness"))]` in `src/lib.rs` with Cargo feature gate + tokio `process/net` features.
- Implemented namespace lifecycle (unique IDs + temp dirs + idempotent cleanup + RAII guard), port reservation allocator with ownership-based release semantics, PG/etcd data-dir prep helpers, async PG16/etcd3 spawners with namespace-local logging, TCP readiness polling, and graceful-first shutdown with forced-kill fallback.
- Added harness tests for uniqueness/idempotent cleanup/concurrent reservations and binary-gated spawn smoke tests; caught and fixed a false-collision test bug by retaining reservations during uniqueness assertions.
- Verification evidence: `cargo test test_harness -- --nocapture` passed; then full gates passed in sequence: `make check`, `make test`, `make test-bdd`, `make lint`.
- Updated task file with skeptical-review amendments, switched to NOW EXECUTE, completed checklist/status tags (`<status>done</status> <passes>true</passes> <passing>true</passing>`), ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, and recorded a new cross-task learning in `AGENTS.md` about keeping port reservations alive in collision tests.
- Next immediate action after this log: stage all modified files including `.ralph` bookkeeping files and commit with required `task finished ...` message.
